closed_children.title: Binary skeleton: CLI, main, participant registration (noop on all commands)

description:
Build the second binary in the workspace: a bidirectional GitHub Issues
plugin that mirrors balls tasks to GitHub Issues (push) and reflects
external GitHub Issue changes back into balls (pull, via the legacy
sync/SyncReport path). Closes the dogfood goal of running balls against
its own issue tracker.

WHY: balls's plugin model unifies push and pull under one Participant
primitive but the wire protocols still split — native propose covers
balls→external push; sync/SyncReport covers external→balls pull. A truly
bidirectional plugin spans both. This epic exercises the participant model
end-to-end and is what proves to operators that "balls is just a
participant; external sources can be authoritative on their own fields"
isn't theoretical.

KEY POLICY DECISIONS (locked in scoping, baked into children):
- Close-mirror is AUTHORITATIVE by default: GH-issue closed → balls task
  status=closed via SyncReport `updated`. Per-event policy still applies;
  operator can override with --skip / per-config best-effort. The strict
  test surface is non-negotiable (B6).
- Auto-create from new GH issues: untagged GH issues become new balls
  tasks via SyncReport `created`. Relies on landed bl-b807 sanitizer and
  bl-4673 ingest backstops in balls core for safety.
- Separate token per plugin: this plugin uses
  .balls/local/plugins/github-issues/, not the forge plugin's auth-dir.
  Allows fine-grained PAT with issues-only scope.

ACCEPTANCE: balls-plugin-github-issues binary passes auth-check; push
half mirrors create/update/close to GH issues idempotently; pull half
correctly maps GH state changes to SyncReport entries (created/updated/
deleted) with loop-avoidance and identity matching; conflict handling
honors the projection rules; live integration test against a sandbox
GitHub repo passes end-to-end.
title: balls-plugin-github-issues: bidirectional GitHub Issues <-> balls plugin
